summary: Tested 5 early warning indicators on synthetic W2S task with controllable bias. Found prediction entropy (r=0.589) and prediction variance (r=-0.581) correlate strongly with generalization failure. All 15 experiments showed complete imitation (>0.8), confirming dominant failure mode. Mean entropy emerges as strongest signal that higher uncertainty during training predicts better final generalization.
claims: Prediction entropy during training correlates positively (r=0.589) with final PGR, suggesting uncertainty maintenance helps avoid premature convergence to biased patterns
Prediction variance correlates negatively (r=-0.581) with PGR, indicating successful students develop more consistent decision boundaries
100% of experiments showed high imitation scores (>0.8), confirming this synthetic setup strongly favors the imitation failure mode
Student-weak disagreement rate shows negative correlation (r=-0.288) with final performance, confirming that less disagreement indicates worse generalization
Critical bias threshold exists around 0.3-0.4 strength where any generalization becomes possible

evidence.summary: Raw experimental results from 15 W2S experiments across 5 bias levels
Statistical analysis of 5 early warning indicators showing correlations with PGR and imitation scores
Complete implementation of synthetic W2S pipeline with controllable bias

limitations: Synthetic task scope limited to 2D XOR classification - real-world applicability uncertain
Small sample size (n=15) limits statistical power and generalizability
Simple logistic regression model may not capture complex failure modes seen in large language models
ROC analysis incomplete due to technical issues with AUC calculation implementation
Goodhart risk: optimizing these indicators could enable gaming without genuine improvement
Potential ground-truth leakage as validation set uses same bias pattern as training data
next: Scale validation to more diverse synthetic tasks (e.g., vision, NLP) and larger model architectures
Fix ROC/AUC calculation to establish proper early warning thresholds and detection accuracy
Develop ensemble indicators combining multiple signals for more robust failure prediction
Test on real weak-to-strong scenarios with actual language model fine-tuning
Investigate dynamic threshold adaptation based on task characteristics and bias patterns
